name: Polaryx Therapeutics, Inc.
business: Polaryx Therapeutics is a clinical-stage biotechnology company developing novel, disease-modifying therapies for rare, pediatric lysosomal storage disorders (LSDs). Its most advanced candidate is PLX-200, an oral small molecule targeting multiple LSDs, including CLN2, CLN3, Krabbe disease, and Sandhoff disease. The company has no approved products and no revenue, relying on equity financings to fund operations.
sellsTo: patients with rare pediatric lysosomal storage disorders
United States, Europe, and select regions of the rest of the world

exposures.sentence: We are a clinical-stage biotechnology company committed to the discovery, development, and commercialization of novel, disease-modifying therapies for rare, pediatric LSDs.

catalysts.event: Initiate Phase 2 SOTERIA basket trial for PLX-200
catalysts.sentence: We expect to initiate this trial in the second half of 2026.

catalysts.event: Direct listing of common stock on Nasdaq
catalysts.sentence: the Direct Listing completed on February 2, 2026

tamClaims.claim: Approximately 50,000 LSD patients in the United States, Europe and select regions of the rest of the world
tamClaims.sentence: We believe that there are approximately 50,000 LSD patients in the United States, Europe and select regions of the rest of the world, assuming an incidence rate of one in 5,000 births.

tamClaims.claim: Roughly 7,700 NCL patients in the United States, Europe and select regions of the rest of the world
tamClaims.sentence: We believe that NCLs represent approximately 15% of the LSD population, roughly 7,700 patients in the United States, Europe and select regions of ROW.

tamClaims.claim: Approximately 6,700 Krabbe disease patients in the United States, Europe and select regions of the rest of the world
tamClaims.sentence: We believe that there are approximately 6,700 Krabbe disease patients in the United States, Europe and select regions of the ROW.

tamClaims.claim: Approximately 1,200 Sandhoff disease patients in the United States, Europe and select regions of the rest of the world
tamClaims.sentence: We believe that there are approximately 1,200 Sandhoff disease patients in the United States, Europe and select regions of the ROW.
